entry.title: How to Pass a Filter Parameter to OData V4 TreeTable Hierarchy Batch Calls in Fiori Elements?
entry.content:
I am building a Fiori Elements List Report page with a TreeTable using the sap.fe.templates.ListReport template and OData V4. The table uses SAP Hierarchy annotations (com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1) with a hierarchyQualifier.

My manifest configuration looks like this:

"controlConfiguration": {
    "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.LineItem": {
        "tableSettings": {
            "type": "TreeTable",
            "hierarchyQualifier": "ResponseHierarchy",
            "selectionMode": "None"
        }
    }
}

The Problem

When the page loads, Fiori Elements automatically fires 3 batch calls:

GET ResponseHierarchy(db05dc96-ea30-4393-a929-266e1c8d2331)
GET ResponseHierarchy/$count
GET ResponseHierarchy?$apply=com.sap.vocabularies.Hierarchy.v1.TopLevels(
    HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy,
    HierarchyQualifier='ResponseHierarchy',
    NodeProperty='ID',
    Levels=1
)&$select=...&$count=true&$skip=0&$top=240

I need to pass a programCycleId to all 3 calls so my backend handler knows which program's data to fetch. The first call works fine because the ID is part of the key segment. However, calls 2 and 3 hit the entity set directly ($root/ResponseHierarchy) without any key or filter.

What I Have Tried

1. Passing ID as route key

{ "pattern": "ResponseHierarchy({ID}):?query:" }

This works for call 1 but calls 2 and 3 don't carry the ID.

2. Passing $filter as query parameter

oParams = { query: { "$filter": "programCycleId eq '" + sProgramId + "'" } };

The $filter in the route URL is not propagated to the $apply=TopLevels(...) and $count calls. The TreeTable control generates these calls referencing $root/ResponseHierarchy directly, completely ignoring the URL $filter.

3. Custom HTTP Header

I ended up using a workaround, setting a custom header x-program-cycle-id on the OData model and reading it in the backend:

oModel.changeHttpHeaders({ "x-program-cycle-id": sProgramCycleId });

This works but it's not ideal because:

  • The header is set globally on the model, affecting all other OData calls
  • It requires manual cache busting (getAllBindings().filter(...).forEach(b => b.refresh()))
  • It's not a clean OData-native solution

My Question

Is there a proper OData V4 / Fiori Elements native way to pass a filter or parameter that gets included in ALL 3 TreeTable hierarchy batch calls, including the $apply=TopLevels(HierarchyNodes=$root/ResponseHierarchy...) call?

Specifically I would like to know:

  1. Is it possible to configure the TreeTable binding via annotations so that a $filter is always appended to all hierarchy calls including the $root/ResponseHierarchy reference inside $apply?

  2. Is there a manifest.json setting or UI5 Fiori Elements API that allows scoping the hierarchy data to a specific filter value without resorting to custom headers?

  3. Is the custom header approach actually the recommended workaround for this scenario, or is there a better pattern?

Any guidance or pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Timesheet Time Entry with Multiple Activity Allocatio...
entry.content:
Hello @S0025758586 

Thank you for your question and the description you provided. Below are answers.

Question: is there such functionality of multiple activity allocation from single timesheet entry supported in standard?

Answer: No, the Public Cloud, one timesheet entry is designed for one activity type posting which is activity-based time recording and does not change automatically into multiple activity allocations.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0bebd08dffca45afa67b1f751199afd0/203be656b9352f7be10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

Question 2: If not, is there a tool to derivate multiple activity allocations with reference to source entry ? 

Answer: Not in the Public Cloud. The standard alternative is either:

  • You enter multiple timesheet lines (T001, T002, T003) that ensures all lines references the same WBS and you make use of copy to reduce your effort (to reduce your user efforts, i recommend you standardise using templates), or
  • If your intention is “indirect cost approximation”, rather than actual secondary activity quantity postings, then you should use standard Overhead Calculation on WBS instead of trying to post multiple types from one record.

Question 3:  is there another procedure under this scope to allocate overheads from cost centers to WBS elements?

Answer: Yes, the available procedural options are:

  • Overhead Calculation on WBS. You can use the overhead calculation apps and configuration.

The following reference guides you on how to run it:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c56f622a2edf491b9f1b596b55587009/b25561e3dd3c44ec846b77d35737bc24.html?locale=en-US

  • Universal Allocation to allocate cost center actuals periodically to WBS by making use of allocation cycles.

See the following KBA for more details.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2860376

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Custom Consignment Process
entry.content:
Following is the requirement that I can see from your question.

You perform Fill up to Customer XX with 130 qty. & customer XX further supplies the product to customers as mentioned below.

1. Customer A - M10 - 100 Qty

2. Customer B - M10 - 10 Qty

3. Customer C - M10 - 20 Qty.

Once these qtys. are sold, you bill to Customer A, B & C. Hope my understanding is right.

In such case, you can play around with the partner functions as below.

1. Consignment Fill-up order - Partner functions SP, SH, BP, PY = Customer XX

2. Consignment Issue order - Partner functions SP & SH = Customer XX || Partner functions BP & PY = Customer A or Customer B or Customer C, depending on who you want to bill.

Thanks, 

Prashant

entry.title: Customized Logo and Splash Screen in MDK Mobile App for Android
entry.content:
This blog explains how to customize the app launcher icon (logo) and the splash screen for an Android customized client built using SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK).

The approach described here is based on real project experience with an MDK customized client built using tools provided by SAP and targeting the Android platform maintained by Google.

When do we need Android branding in MDK?

You normally customize the Android logo and splash screen when:

  • you build a customized MDK client

  • your customer requires corporate branding

  • the app will be distributed internally or through an app store

This is different from a standard MDK client where you cannot change native branding.

 

Android branding folder in MDK customized app

All Android-specific branding is handled inside:

\project_folder\App_Resources_Merge\Android\src\main\res

You should only work inside the native resource folders and only replace images.

Customizing the Android App Logo (Launcher Icon) :

In Android, the launcher icon is maintained using the standard Android mipmap folders.

In an MDK customized app, go to:

App_Resources/Android/src/main/res/

You will find folders such as (if not create the folders):

  • mipmap-mdpi

  • mipmap-hdpi

  • mipmap-xhdpi

  • mipmap-xxhdpi

  • mipmap-xxxhdpi

Inside each folder, there will already be an existing launcher icon file ( if not generate the icon file from android studio asset).

What you should do

  • Replace the image file with your customer logo

  • Keep:

    • the same file name

    • the same folder structure

What you should not do

  • Do not rename the icon files

  • Do not delete any mipmap folders

  • Do not change any Android manifest or build files

MDK and the Android project already reference these resource names internally.

It will look like this after adding the icon: 

Customizing the Android Splash Screen

The splash screen is the first screen shown when the MDK client starts.

In an Android MDK customized app, the splash screen image is usually stored under:

App_Resources_Merge/Android/src/main/res/drawable/

You will see a default splash image used by the MDK client.

How to customize

  • Replace the existing splash image with your branded image

  • Keep the same file name

If your project uses a background color together with the splash image, the color is usually defined in the Android style configuration (for example in ic_launcher_background.xml).
In most MDK projects, you do not need to modify this unless your branding requires a different background color.

After adding drawable folder and logo , following structure will be like this : 

Important rule for Android MDK branding

In an MDK customized Android app:

Only replace image resources.

Do not modify:

  • native Java / Kotlin files

  • AndroidManifest.xml

  • Gradle configuration

  • MDK internal resources

Changing anything beyond images can cause build failures or runtime issues during future MDK or Android upgrades.

 

Conclusion

In an Android customized MDK client, changing the app logo and splash screen is a simple and safe activity if done correctly.

The key point is:

For Android MDK branding, only replace the existing image resources under
App_Resources/Android/src/main/res/ and never change native configuration or code files.

Following this approach ensures that your branded MDK Android app remains stable and upgrade-safe.

entry.title: Re: Parent–Child Material Sales Scenario for MTS (Make-to...
entry.content:
Possible Approaches in Public Cloud

1) Sales BOM + Material Determination
Use the parent material as the order entry point. Configure material determination rules so that if one child is not available, another child can be substituted. This requires predefined substitution logic (e.g., child A → child B → child C).

2) Advanced ATP (aATP)
In S/4HANA Cloud, aATP offers product substitution and alternative materials functionality. You can define Product Substitution Lists so that if the requested material is not available, the system proposes alternatives. This is the closest fit to your requirement, but it still requires configuration of substitution rules — it won’t dynamically pick “any child with stock” unless you define the substitution sequence.

Recommendation

  • Activate scope item 4A6 (Advanced ATP) in your Public Cloud tenant.
  • Define Product Substitution Lists for your parent material, mapping to child materials.
  • Configure substitution priority so the system checks stock sequentially.
  • Train users to expect that the sales order will show the substituted child material, not the parent.

entry.title: Re: Purchasing currency USD invoice paid in EUR base curr...
entry.content:
How SAP handles this

1) Invoice Posting (USD → INR)
When you post the vendor invoice, SAP records the amount in USD and translates it into INR using the exchange rate valid on the invoice date. This establishes the liability in both USD and INR.

2) Payment Posting (EUR → INR)
When you pay in EUR, SAP records the outgoing payment in EUR and translates it into INR using the exchange rate valid on the payment date. The system then clears the USD liability against the EUR payment.

3) Currency Reconciliation
Because the invoice was in USD and the payment was in EUR, SAP automatically calculates the exchange rate difference in INR. This difference is posted to the Exchange Rate Difference account (configured in OB09 for FI). The reconciliation ensures that the vendor account is cleared, and any FX gain/loss is recognized in INR.

entry.title: Re: How to access Withholding Tax Summaries and Cumulativ...
entry.content:
Good day @JuanseVal 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, withholding tax reporting and reconciliation is primarily managed through Document and Reporting Compliance using Run Statutory Reports, and the Manage Withholding Tax Items capability is typically an embedded activity inside DRC, not a standalone tile you can open directly.

The Public Cloud standardly provides a legal and reconciliation-oriented withholding tax output via DRC with Run Statutory Reports and a controlled reporting framework where report definitions and executions are managed centrally.

So, if you need a very specific breakdown “by Colombia tax indicator” and a custom grouping layout, the usual best practice is to use DRC for the statutory run, then build a controlled reconciliation view using analytics (though only if your tenant exposes the needed fields).

Here are answers to your questions:

Question 1: What is the exact Business Role and Business Catalog required in release 2602 to access withholding tax summaries for Colombia?

Answer: In Public Cloud, the reliable way is for you to assign the DRC statutory reporting catalogs to a business role that you maintain in Maintain Business Roles, then assign it to your user. The key catalogs you commonly require for your scenario are:

  • SAP_FIN_BC_SRF_COMMON_PC (This is required for running statutory reports authorisations). See KBA 3587016 for more insights.
  • SAP_FIN_BC_SRF_RUN_PC (This Blog: Review and Adapt Business Roles after a Major Upgrade in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides more details)
  • SAP_FIN_BC_MWTI_COMMON_PC (See KBA 3503880 and the blog above for more details).

A behaviour you should be aware of is, even with the catalog assigned, F5428 may not show in Launchpad search because it is not a standalone tile because you access it through navigation from Run Statutory Reports. So, the exact role is typically a customer role that contain the catalogs i mentioned above, rather than one single universal role name.

Question 2: Is the app F5428 the standard tool for this version, or has it been replaced/integrated into the Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) framework (e.g., via Run Statutory Reports - F1515)?

Answer: For Public Cloud, the standard path is to use DRC framework with Runs Statutory Reports as the entry point and “Manage Withholding Tax Items” will appear as an embedded reporting activity where applicable.

So, it is not “replaced” as in removed but it is operationally integrated into DRC usage and you may not be able to directly launch it.

See the following references for more details:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3503880

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/fb15635742dced74e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

Question 3: Are there any specific SSCUI configurations (like activating the Withholding Tax Reporting Date - 105376) that are mandatory to make these records visible in the reporting apps?

Answer: Yes, this can happen when withholding tax items are not visible or not selectable by the period you expect. KBA 3655186 and KBA 3680198 highlight that you must activate the Withholding Tax Reporting Date and set the reporting date basis. If this is not active or not populated, selections and reporting can behave as not expected.

Also for 2602 release, note that enhanced configuration options for withholding tax reporting indicates ongoing improvements around how withholding tax reporting is controlled.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/aaede248b98d4ab48ea3abc032e33911.html?locale=en-US

In your system, search for 105376 (Configure Your Solution), check the following:

  • Withholding tax reporting date is activated and enabled.
  • Reporting date source is maintained.

If your system already has postings without the reporting date being populated, you may need a migration style follow up step to populate it. See KBA 3655186 for more guidance on this.

Below is best practice recommendation for your reconciliation before you submit in Public Cloud

  1. Use Run Statutory Reports to run the relevant Colombia withholding tax reporting task for the period.
  2. Use the report output and built in drilldown options as your statutory reconciliation backbone.
  3. For you to be able to trace document level, use journal entry display apps to open the accounting documents that are referenced by the statutory run. This will depend on the output your Colombia report provides.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4de200b26d5d42bbacae69c6bf6b9923/fb15635742dced74e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/64877a1c4d084150a19c843d241c9df2/4bdc76b44b3f452db78278ddddc9f56e.html?locale=en-US

If the standard DRC output does not provide your exact grouping in one view, the recommended Public Cloud approach is to:

  • Keep DRC as the legal source of truth for your reporting
  • Build a controlled reconciliation report using analytics only if the required withholding tax fields are exposed in released content.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Scaling Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA: Enterprise-Ready Innovation with ISLM
entry.content:
• Introduction and context
  • Key Enhancements for Generative AI Enablement in ISLM
  • Availability and Downport Strategy Across SAP S/4HANA Releases
  • Deep Dive: Latest ISLM Capabilities for Generative AI
  • Orchestration Service Integration
  • Developing Custom Generative AI Use Cases — Step-by-Step guide
  • Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub
  • Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications in SAP ABAP Cloud with ISLM and ABAP AI SDK
  • Conclusion

Introduction and context

In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, Generative AI  is transforming how enterprises innovate, automate, and deliver business value . To successfully embed Generative AI within SAP S/4HANA, organizations need more than access to large language models. They require a structured, scalable, and governed framework to integrate, operate, and manage AI use cases across their landscape.

Over the past year, these Generative AI capabilities are now enabled through ISLM, helping customers and partners move from experimentation to productive usage. You can learn more through my previous blog post and the  YouTube webinar replay which outline the architecture, supported use cases, and how ISLM integrates Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA SAP business applications. 

Building on this foundation, this blog focuses on the latest and upcoming enhancements in Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management that further strengthen Generative AI enablement. We will explore what’s new, how these enhancements simplify GenAI integration and lifecycle management, and how customers and partners can leverage them to accelerate innovation and scale AI-driven business scenarios in SAP S/4HANA.

Key Enhancements for Generative AI Enablement in ISLM

  1. Integration with the Orchestration Service:  ISLM now integrates with the Orchestration Service to deliver advanced, enterprise-grade  capabilities for Generative AI scenarios. This includes Document Grounding to enrich prompts with business-relevant context, Content Filtering to ensure safe and compliant responses, and Data Masking to protect sensitive information before it is processed by large language models. In addition, the service supports input and output translation for multilingual use cases and incorporates a Prompt Shield filter to help defend against prompt injection attacks. Together, these capabilities significantly enhance the security, reliability, and governance of Generative AI embedded within SAP business applications.
  2. Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub: ISLM enables customers to leverage state-of-the-art large language models while maintaining flexibility and future readiness.
  3. Reuse Connectivity to Gen AI Hub:  A shared connectivity model allows a single connection between the ABAP system and
  4. the SAP Generative AI Hub to be configured once and reused across multiple AI use cases. This simplifies administration, improves governance, and reduces setup effort.
  5. Simplification of creation of Intelligent Scenario: Creation of Intelligent Scenarios has been simplified, moving from a pro-code approach to a more streamlined low-code experience.In addition, ISLM allows the optional use of the prediction class for Generative AI scenarios. 

Availability and Downport Strategy Across SAP S/4HANA Releases 

To accelerate the adoption of Generative AI, the latest ISLM enhancements have been downported to lower SAP S/4HANA releases, enabling customers to benefit from new capabilities without waiting for a full system upgrade. These enhancements are available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA On-Premise.

Supported Releases (No TCI Required)

For the following Support Package (SP) levels, the latest capabilities are already included — no additional TCI implementation is required:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01
  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS06

Lower SP Levels (TCI Required)

For lower SP levels within the 2025 and 2023 releases, the relevant Transport-based Correction Instructions (TCIs) must be implemented to enable the latest ISLM capabilities.

By delivering these enhancements via TCIs, customers can adopt the newest ISLM innovations without requiring a complete Support Package update. This approach significantly reduces upgrade complexity while providing faster access to the latest Generative AI functionality in ISLM.

To enable the new capabilities, please implement the corresponding SAP Notes listed below.

  • SAP Note 3682148
  • SAP Note 3668194

Deep Dive: Latest ISLM Capabilities for Generative AI

Orchestration Service Integration

ISLM supports integration with the Orchestration Service on SAP AI Core, enabling advanced Generative AI capabilities such as document grounding, content filtering, and data masking. Additional features like input and output translation and a Prompt Shield filter help protect against prompt injection attacks, strengthening security and trust. New API enhancements further improve grounding quality through larger chunk sizes and application-specific document chunking. Together, these capabilities enable standardized, flexible, and enterprise-ready GenAI integration—allowing customers to switch between foundation models without code changes and reinforcing ISLM as the central framework for governing and scaling Generative AI across SAP landscapes.

The following modules within the orchestration service are supported by ISLM :

  1. Data Masking – Protects sensitive information by masking or removing confidential data.
  2. Input Filtering – Validates and sanitizes incoming data before it is processed by the LLM.
  3. Output Filtering – Reviews and filters the LLM-generated output to ensure compliance and relevance.
  4. Grounding – Aligns model outputs with enterprise data or domain-specific knowledge to improve response accuracy.
Developing Custom Generative AI Use Cases — Step-by-Step guide

Following information is relevant for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition & On-Premise releases 

For step-by-step guide on using orchestration within ISLM, let’s consider the following example use case:

Use Case: AI-Driven Return Policy Analysis with ISLM

In this scenario, two fictional sports brands — Company Alpha and Company Beta — maintain different return policies with varying eligibility rules, timelines, refund conditions, and exceptions.

Customer support teams frequently handle queries such as:

  • “Is this item still eligible for return?”

  • “Are customized products refundable?”

  • “How long will the refund take?”

Manually reviewing policy documents is time-consuming and can lead to inconsistent responses.

Using ISLM with orchestration:

  • Policy documents are uploaded for document grounding.

  • User queries are processed through the orchestration workflow.

  • The LLM extracts relevant clauses and generates structured responses.

Business Value

  • Reduces manual effort

  • Speeds up response time

  • Ensures consistent policy interpretation

  • Enables automated decision support within business processes

Implementation Steps 

  1. Set and configure SAP AI Core
  2. Design and development of the Orchestration Workflow
  3. Export workflow configuration
  4. Create and deploy Intelligent Scenarios 
  5. Consumption of the Intelligent Scenario with ABAP AI SDK APIs

Prerequisites for using Gen AI scenario in ISLM. Ensure that you have completed the following steps before continuing with creating your own Gen AI scenario: Prerequisites | SAP Help Portal

1) Access AI Launchpad: Design and Development of the Orchestration Workflow
Open an available instance of the SAP Generative AI Hub – >AI Launchpad to start prompt engineering and configure your orchestration workflow.

1.1) Configure Orchestration Modules
Within the AI Launchpad, configure the required modules for the orchestration service according to your use case, refer to the following link:  Build Your Orchestration Workflow | SAP Help Portal

Sample Orchestration Configuration 

{
	"modules": {
		"grounding": {
			"type": "document_grounding_service",
			"config": {
				"filters": [],
				"placeholders": {
					"input": [
						"grounding_input_variable_1"
					],
					"output": "grounding_output_variable"
				}
			}
		},
		"prompt_templating": {
			"prompt": {
				"template": [
					{
						"role": "user",
						"content": [
							{
								"type": "text",
								"text": "{{?grounding_output_variable}}"
							}
						]
					}
				],
				"defaults": {}
			},
			"model": {
				"name": "gpt-5-mini",
				"params": {
					"max_tokens": 64000,
					"temperature": 1
				},
				"version": "1"
			}
		},
		"filtering": {
			"input": {
				"filters": [
					{
						"type": "azure_content_safety",
						"config": {
							"hate": 2,
							"self_harm": 2,
							"sexual": 2,
							"violence": 2
						}
					},
					{
						"type": "llama_guard_3_8b",
						"config": {
							"child_exploitation": true,
							"code_interpreter_abuse": true,
							"defamation": true,
							"elections": true,
							"hate": true,
							"indiscriminate_weapons": true,
							"intellectual_property": true,
							"non_violent_crimes": true,
							"privacy": true,
							"self_harm": true,

			},

				"config": {
					"source_language": "",
					"target_language": "USER_LANG"
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

It is mandatory to have a valid orchestration configuration as a workflow. Ensure the orchestration workflow is configured and exported as a JSON file. Refer to the above section.

1.2) Export Workflow Configuration
After all necessary modules have been configured, export the orchestration workflow configuration as a JSON file for use or deployment.

2) Reuse Connectivity

A shared connectivity model allows a single connection between the ABAP system and the SAP Generative AI Hub to be configured once and reused across multiple AI use cases. This simplifies administration, improves governance, and reduces setup effort.

Maintain Intelligent Scenario Usage Type Connections | SAP Help Portal

 3) Create the Intelligent Scenario

3.1) Launch the SAP Fiori Launchpad and open the Intelligent Scenarios app.

Choose Create → Side-by-Side, then in the Settings tab:

  • Enter name and description
  • Select Generative AI as the Scenario Type
  • ISLM allows optional use of the prediction class for Generative AI scenarios 
  • Select Stateless – Customer as the Usage Type, which points to the reuse connectivity created in the previous step.

SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help

 3.2)  Add Generative AI Model to the Intelligent scenario

Choose Add Model and maintain the required details:

  • Enter a model name and description
  • Select the appropriate Executable ID and corresponding model
  • (Optional) Choose a specific model version (latest is selected by default)
  • Optionally add a prompt template
  • Choose Add to confirm

3.3) Intelligent scenario with Orchestration service

    • Upload  the orchestration Execution Flow JSON in this model to integrate the orchestration service.
    • Use the Upload button to add the execution flow template or Copy the JSON into the dialog.  

  • Navigate to the Execution Flow Template
    • Once a valid JSON is uploaded, the modules will be displayed with their parameters and values in display mode.

In the Grounding subsection, add the required data repository types (vector, help.sap.com)

To restrict your grounding module output during inference, maintain Search Configuration value. This can help to retrieve only relevant data instead of all the uploaded data.

The Input Translation module allows you to translate LLM text prompts and grounding module output into a target language. It may help improve LLM response when the configured model performs better with input in specific language, example English.

The Data Masking module enables the anonymization or pseudonymization of data before it is sent to the LLM model for processing.

The input filters defined in the Execution Flow Template JSON are populated and displayed in the table.

The Output Filtering module allows you to filter the harmful or hateful content generated by the LLM. 

The Output Translation module allows you to translate LLM response into a target language. It helps to display the LLM response in the language user logged, if the translation is supported.

3.4) Add Prompt Template

 

 Add the grounding template

Add a Grounding Template. The grounding template can include dynamic parameters similar to the user prompt.

Add Prompt Template | SAP Help Portal

The Grounding Template retrieves relevant information from the selected data repository and appends it to the user prompt. It supports dynamic parameters, which are provided at runtime (during inference).

Add user prompt

If you have configured grounding module, at least one User Prompt must be added with the following parameter: {ISLM_GROUNDING_OUTPUT}

At runtime, this placeholder is automatically replaced with the content retrieved by the Grounding Template from the configured data repository.

add-prompt-template| SAP Help Portal 

3.6) Upload grounding documents and publish Intelligent scenario

 

Upload documents to an Intelligent Scenario, refer to the following link: SAP Help Portal | Upload Grounding Document

Below are sample return policy documents for Company Alpha and Company Beta that you can use for this example use case.

You can copy the content into separate .txt files (for example: company_alpha_return_policy.txt and company_beta_return_policy.txt) and upload them to your orchestration workflow for document grounding.

ompany Alpha Return Policy

**Eligibility:**
- Items must be purchased from Alpha.com, Alpha App, or Alpha stores.
- Items must be in original condition, unworn and unwashed.
- Alpha By You custom products are eligible for return within the return window.

**Time Frame:**
- Standard items: 60 days from purchase date.
- Holiday purchases may have extended return windows.

**Condition of Items:**
- Items must include original packaging and accessories.
- Apparel must have tags attached.

**Refund Methods:**
- Refunds issued to original payment method.
- PayPal in-store purchases refunded via Alpha gift card.
- Refunds typically processed within 10 days of receipt.

**Exceptions:**
- Items from Alpha Clearance stores are not eligible.
- Items purchased from other retailers cannot be returned to Alpha.

**Return Process:**
1. Initiate return via Alpha account order history.
2. Print prepaid UPS label or use QR code.
3. Drop off at UPS or schedule pickup.
4. Refund issued after inspection.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Company Beta Return Policy

**Eligibility:**
- Items must be purchased from us.Beta.com or Beta retail stores.
- Custom products are nonrefundable unless defective.
- Gift cards and bodywear including face masks are not eligible for return.

**Time Frame:**
- Standard items: 45 days from the date of purchase.
- MB.01/MB.02 Basketball Shoes: 15 days.


**Condition of Items:**
- Items must be unused and in original packaging.
- Include original form of payment and order confirmation for store returns.

**Refund Methods:**
- Refunds are issued to the original payment method.
- Beta Gift Card refunds are issued as separate E-Gift Cards.
- Refunds may take 1–2 weeks to process.

**Exceptions:**
- No returns on gift cards, customized products, and bodywear.
- No free returns for APO/FPO addresses and US Territories.

**Return Process:**
1. Initiate return via Beta returns portal.
2. Use pre-paid shipping label provided.
3. Drop off at designated location.
4. Refund processed upon receipt and inspection.

4) Deploy & Activate

Once the Intelligent Scenario is created, we can perform operations such as deployment and activation using Intelligent Scenario Management App.

Click Deploy.

You can now activate the deployment to consume the inference in your business application. You can activate deployment for your user or all users by selecting the For Me and For All options respectively.

5) Consumption of Intelligent Scenario with ABAP AI SDK

Leverage the Intelligent Scenario, together with the defined orchestration workflow, to drive automated decision-making within your business processes.

The ABAP AI SDK provides dedicated APIs to seamlessly consume inference and prompt execution results directly within your ABAP application logic.

Prompt Execution with Orchestration Service

Use the intelligent scenario within the orchestration to drive decision-making and automation.

Once the selected deployment is activated, you can use ABAP AI SDK for inference consumption. For reference, see Utilization Of The Orchestration Service Of The Generative AI Hub.

Sample Code

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report Z_TEST_ISLM_REPORT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT Z_TEST_ISLM_REPORT.

PARAMETERS: company TYPE string OBLIGATORY.

CONSTANTS lv_scenario TYPE islm_de_sbs_is_name VALUE 'Z_ORCHESTRATION_SCEN'.

TRY.

    " Create an instance of the ABAP GenAI SDK based on an ISLM Scenario
    FINAL(api) = cl_aic_islm_orch_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance( lv_scenario ).

    api->configure_grounding( )->add_grounding_template( template_id = 'GROUNDING_TEMPLATE'
                                                         template_parameters = VALUE #( ( name = 'ISLM_GROUNDING_QUERY'
                                                                                          value = company ) ) ).

    api->configure_templating( )->add_prompt_template( message_type = aic_prompt_message_type=>user_message
                                                       template_id  = 'SUMMARIZE_RETURN_POLICY' ).

    api->set_input_parameter( name  = 'ISLM_COMPANY_NAME'
                              value = company ).

    " execute
    FINAL(response) = api->execute( ).

    " get the completion
    FINAL(answer) = response->orchestration_result( )->completion( ).

    DATA(lv_status) = 'S'.

    cl_demo_output=>display_text( text = answer ).

  CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(lx_factory).
    DATA(error) = lx_factory->get_text( ).
    lv_status = 'E'.
    cl_demo_output=>display_text( text = error ).
  CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(lx_completion).
    DATA(error2) = lx_completion->get_text( ).
    lv_status = 'E'.
    cl_demo_output=>display_text( text = error2 ).
  CATCH cx_aic_prompt_template INTO DATA(lx_prompt_template).
    DATA(error3) = lx_prompt_template->get_text( ).
    lv_status = 'E'.
    cl_demo_output=>display_text( text = error3 ).
  CATCH cx_aic_orchestration_api INTO DATA(lx_orchestration).
    DATA(error4) = lx_orchestration->get_text( ).
    lv_status = 'E'.
    cl_demo_output=>display_text( text = error4 ).
ENDTRY.

Run the sample report with Company = COMPANY AlPHA

The output is a summary generated by large language models (LLMs).

Support for new models offered by Generative AI Hub

With the ABAP AI SDK, powered by ISLM, support has been extended to large language models (LLMs), including the following:

gpt-5
gpt-5-mini
gpt-5-nano
o3
o4-mini
gemini-2.5-flash
gemini-2.5-pro
amazon--nova-pro
amazon--nova-lite
amazon--nova-micro
mistralai--mistral-medium-instruct
mistralai--mistral-small-instruct

This enhancement broadens model support, enabling alignment with varied enterprise requirements.

For detailed information on supported models, availability, please refer to the relevant SAP Note 3668194 .

Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications in SAP ABAP Cloud with ISLM and ABAP AI SDK 

By leveraging ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM), you can enrich your custom ABAP applications with seamlessly integrated Generative AI capabilities. This enables you to embed intelligent, context-aware features directly into your business processes while maintaining governance and clean core principles. This approach enables customers to extend SAP applications in a future-proof and upgrade-safe manner. 

Together, ISLM and ABAP AI SDK offer a powerful yet governed framework for embedding Generative AI into ABAP applications, ensuring enterprise-level reliability, security, and scalability. 

For detailed  guidance and  steps, refer to the SAP Help Portal | Developing Your Own AI-Enabled Applications: 

To get started, refer to the SAP Help Portal  .

Conclusion

Generative AI delivers real enterprise value when it is embedded directly into business processes with the right governance, scalability, and control. Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management  provides exactly that foundation, enabling structured creation, orchestration, deployment, and lifecycle management of AI scenarios within SAP S/4HANA.

With advanced runtime capabilities such as Document Grounding, Content Filtering, Data Masking, multilingual support,  reusable connectivity, and expanded model support, ISLM transforms Generative AI from experimentation into operational reality. Get started today and take your Generative AI innovation to the next level with ISLM!!

entry.title: To run production process in sap public cloud what are the config are required for EWM
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Requirement:
In our process, production is executed in the IM storage location, while the raw materials are stored at the warehouse level (EWM‑managed). Once a production order is released, the picking process must be initiated so that the required stock can be picked from the warehouse and moved to the IM production storage location.

Expected Process Flow:
After the production order is released, the system should trigger the picking process. Once picking is completed, the system should automatically create the corresponding delivery document or warehouse task. This will allow the warehouse operator to print the pick slip, locate the materials in the warehouse, and pick the required quantities.

I am currently following the 5HO process, but at the step “Stage Material for Production Order Level”, the system is not fetching the production order number.

I request guidance on the configuration steps required in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to enable this process. Kindly share the required configuration details or documentation.

Thank you,
Kiran

entry.title: Assistance Required for SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) & Scale‑Up High Availability Configuration
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have completed the installation of the SAP HANA database on both the primary node (currently running with business data) and the secondary node (with no business data).

I am seeking your assistance in configuring SAP PERSON_NAME System Replication (HSR) and setting up a Scale‑Up High Availability (HA) environment for our SAP HANA database.

At present, neither the system replication nor the HA setup has been configured. We request your guidance and support to complete the configuration according to SAP best practices.

We request SAP’s support for the following activities:

  1. Guidance on configuring SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) between the primary and secondary nodes.
  2. Assistance with the correct configuration sequence, including:
    • Initial data load
    • Secondary node registration
    • Activation of replication
    • Validation and health checks
  3. Recommendations for Scale‑Up High Availability setup, including best practices for:
    • Replication mode (sync / syncmem / async)
    • Operation mode (logreplay)
    • Cluster integration with Pacemaker (if applicable)
  4. Verification that the planned architecture is aligned with SAP recommendations and supported configurations.
  5. Clarification on any prerequisite checks, logs, or additional information required from our side to proceed.

We are ready to provide diagnostic logs, system replication outputs, landscape information, or any other details upon request.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required for SAP HANA System Replication (...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please take a look at the answers below for your questions.

1.Guidance on configuring SAP HANA System Replication (HSR) between the primary and secondary nodes.
A: Please refer to 'General Prerequisites for Configuring SAP HANA System Replication' in System Replication guide. 

2.Assistance with the correct configuration sequence, including:
A: You can set up the System Replication in various ways. 
   Please refer to 'Configuring SAP HANA System Replication' in System Replication guide. 
   
   '3. Which indications exist for problems with the SAP HANA system replication?' KBA 1999880 - FAQ:SAP HANA System Replication.
   
3.Recommendations for Scale Up High Availability setup, including best practices for:
A: Please refer to the following documents and KBA.
   Replication Modes for SAP HANA System Replication 
   Operation Modes for SAP HANA System Replication 
   '35. What is the logreplay operation mode?' in KBA 1999880 - FAQ:SAP HANA System Replication. 
   

4.Verification that the planned architecture is aligned with SAP recommendations and supported configurations.
A: The current OS version SLES 15 SP3 is supported with HANA 2.0 SPS07.
   For details, please refer to KBA 2235581 - SAP HANA: Supported Operating Systems. 

5.Clarification on any prerequisite checks, logs, or additional information required from our side to proceed.
A: Please refer to the answer 1 above.
   You can see the details about the prerequisites for configuring System Replication there.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Installation of Crystal Reports 2025 damages installation of Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39
entry.content:
We have this behavior on several development PCs.

What was installed:

  • Crystal Report 2020 SP05
  • Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39 (32 bit)

Before installing CR 2025 we uninstalled CR 2020 (and its SPs).
After installing CR 2025 (RTM Patch 800) we started our ERP program.

When trying to print we got the following exception:
Message: TypeInitializationException: The type initializer for 'CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument' threw an exception.
Source: CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine

Message: FileNotFoundException: Retrieving the COM class factory for component with CLSID {4DB2E2BB-78E6-4AEA-BEFB-FDAAB610FD1B} failed due to the following error: 8007007e The specified module could not be found. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x8007007E).
Source: CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine

Workaround: when we install "Crystal Reports for Visual Studio SP 39" again, the exception no longer comes.

So there should be a compatibility problem between CR 2025 and CRVS SP39?

Regards,
Patrick

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: Re: How to check the change log of sales prices 'Manage P...
entry.content:
It is not able to check the change log in Manage Prices - Sales app at present.

An alternative approach that may help you achieve your goal:  

  1. Open "Set Material- Prices" app.
  2. Fill out Condition Type and click Key Combination, then choose.
  3. Fill out all the required entry fields, then click Execute.
  4. Select the condition records.
  5. In the upper menu click More-> Environment -> Changes -> Per condition record/Change record, then it will be able to see the change log of each record.

Refer KBA 3135462 - How to check the change log of sales prices - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

entry.title: Introducing prebuilt Joule skills in SAP Build digital workspaces: Get more done with simple prompts
entry.content:
If your day involves spinning up new spaces, hunting for the right workspace, or sharing content with your team, you know the clicks can add up. SAP Build simplifies this in its digital workspace experience with new prebuilt Joule skills which turns plain‑language prompts into actions. Ask Joule to create or find a workspace and it does the work in the background- then gives you a direct link in the chat so you can jump right in

The first set of prebuilt Joule skills are already live. Take a look at what’s available today: 

 

What’s in this first set of skills

  • Create workspaces from a prompt
    • Example: “Create a workspace called ‘Q3 Onboarding Hub’ using the standard onboarding template.”
    • Joule creates and names the workspace behind the scenes and returns a link in the chat.
  • Find workspaces you need
    • Example: “Show me all workspaces I’m a member of related to ‘Onboarding’.”
    • Joule lists relevant workspaces right in the chat, helping you get where you need to go faster.
  • Add documents right from chat
    • Drag and drop a file into the Joule chat, confirm the target workspace, and Joule adds it for you- no tab hopping required.
  • Find and add members to workspaces
    • Tell Joule who you want to add to a workspace, and Joule finds the person, confirms and sends them an invitation to join the workspace.

Why this matters

For teams needing a space to collaborate and produce results, these skills simplify their day-to-day so that -

  • members spend less time on common setup tasks like launching a project hub, onboarding center, or campaign space,
  • users can move from “search and navigate” to “ask and act” without leaving their flow of work,
  • workspace creators get consistency in content through standardized workspace templates to get started quickly, 
  • business users get more self-service options and admins get fewer “how do I create/find X?” tickets.

Prebuilt skills can be used in conjunction with the existing AI capabilities already available in digital workspaces. For example, once you find or create workspaces via Joule, you can use embedded AI assistance to create and publish content or summarize documents within the workspace.

Further, Joule can be enabled with document grounding for workspaces so that it answers questions based on content maintained in workspaces and links back to sources. This way its responses are grounded and relevant to business context.

Admins and developers can also build custom skills for Joule using Joule Studio and use them in digital workspaces.

Everyday scenarios where you can use these skills:

  • HR onboarding hub: “Create a workspace called ‘New Hire Hub – July’ based on the onboarding template.” Share the link with HR/ team managers in minutes.
  • Project kickoff: “Find all workspaces mentioning ‘Apollo Launch’.” Jump straight into the right workspace for plans, assets, and status.
  • Policy refresh: “Create a workspace named ‘FY26 Policy Updates’.” Drag and drop updated PDFs into the chat to populate the workspace content folder quickly.
  • Campaign workspace: “Create ‘Q2 Partner Enablement’.” Then use AI writing assistance on the landing page to publish a clean, on‑brand overview.
  • Knowledge triage: “Show my ‘Customer Escalations’ workspaces.” Get a list in chat and dive into the relevant one to update content or add the right members to solve the issue.

Getting started with Joule’s prebuilt skills and embedded AI in digital workspaces:

    • First, the admin must ensure Work Zone is enabled as a Joule launcher as per the instructions in this guide
    • Once set up is done, open Joule in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition or SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone and try prompts like
      • “Create a workspace named ‘Team All‑Hands’.”
      • “Find my workspaces related to ‘Compliance’.”
    • Ask your admin to enable document grounding for the workspaces you rely on- so Joule can answer questions with citations.
    • Ask your admin to enable embedded AI features to use AI assistance when editing pages or posts to speed up content creation once your workspace is ready or to summarize your documents quickly. See how you can use embedded AI for document summarization or workspace content creation here: 

 

Tips for Best Results for interacting with Joule:

  • Be specific with search keywords while searching for workspaces or content with Joule
  • While prompting give sufficient context to help Joule understand the task.

What’s next
This is just the first set of prebuilt skills we’re releasing for digital workspaces. We’re continuing to expand the catalog with more actions and smarter multi-step flows.

entry.title: S4HANA Cloud - Fiori Quick Deploy - Change the tile title
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP community,

I am facing a quite funny issue. I have deployed a Fiori RAP application on the stack of a S4HANA Cloud system (Public) via the Quick Deploy function in ADT. After while the key user decided, the Fiori tile is not correct and want me to change it.

Unfortunately (from some mysterious reason) the Launchpad App Descriptor object (which contains the needed data) is not editable in Eclipse ADT, when I try to change the field Title in the Tile Details I get this error:

I tried also use the Maintain Translation app to translate it to second language and then from the second language to the source language, but the source language texts cannot be changed. 

Does anyone have an idea how to proceed? Last solution for me is to create a new project in BAS and deploy it again from BAS with the correct title, but that's too complicated for the fact I want to change just the Fiori tile text. I also cannot redeploy it from ADT, it seems the Quick Deploy can be executed only once per service binding.

 

Thank you in advance,

Matus

entry.title: Re: Intrastat Region
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

I've a follow-up question related to the second error. We've been activating RITA so BE is our plant abroad. If I access SSCUI 102191 which is the place I bealive we should use for adding the region I can see that the different regions available for BE is as follow:

entry.title: Re: Data Integration in ABAP Cloud: Data Exposure using SQL services
entry.content:
Hello @MioYasutake 

happy to hear that you find it useful! hope you find your answers below:

1. The service definition expose the RAP BO to the consumer and then you need to define a service binding of type SQL exposes RAP entities as SQL-accessible artifacts at the database layer. 
S_SQL_VIEW is the SAP authorization object that controls which database views can be accessed via Open SQL. The field SQL_SCHEMA in that object is used to restrict the database schema of the SQL view that a user is allowed to access. So, the value for SQL_SCHEMA is your service binding  ZSQL_TRAVEL_000 .

2. You need to export the SAP system’s public certificate from the PSE and give that to the HANA  so it can trust your SAP system. You can download the Certificate from "Maintain Client Certificates"  Fiori application in your system's UI. 

Regards

Safa

entry.title: Can a username be set as login identifier in SAP CDC?
entry.content:
SAP CDC (Gigya) typically enforces email as a unique identifier for accounts. However we have following business requirement:

  • A single email ID may be reused by multiple users across the system.
  • Each user will have a different username, even though the email address is the same.
  • Example scenario:
    • A company admin role is assigned to different employees over time.
    • The company ID remains the same, but the username changes based on the employee.
    • The email ID must not be treated as unique, because the company uses a shared/functional email address (e.g., admin@company.com).

Is it technically possible in SAP CDC to allow multiple accounts with the same email address but different usernames?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can a username be set as login identifier in SAP CDC?
entry.content:
In SAP CDC email is not necessarily unique. You can set either Username or Email as login identifier.

1. Go to CDC console > Connect > Self Registration > In settings you can choose your login identifier. For your usecase 'Username'

2. Add username field in Registration and Login screens.

Now endusers can register with same email address and different usernames.

Documentation: Login Identifier

entry.title: No Access to SAP Cloud CBC Demo
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have purchased the SAP Cloud ERP Public Edition demo system for 3 months (Cloud test, demo, and development for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, one-system landscape):
https://www.sap.com/germany/products/erp/s4hana.html

We received the Identity Authentication Service (IAS) tenant from SAP, and we can log in there and create users and administrators. We can also access the Cloud ERP Public Edition demo system and create users, roles, etc.

However, we cannot access the Central Business Configuration (CBC) system. Even though we have assigned the administrators in IAS with all CBC roles, when we open the CBC link we receive the error message “Unauthorized.”

On the page “Cloud Identity Services – Tenants”, we noticed that SAP has automatically bundled the CBC demo system with an old IPS tenant from 2016.

 

Because of this, we also do not have the following option available in IAS?
Under Administrator → “Manage Identity Provisioning.”

 

 

 

 

Although we already have all administrator roles and groups in BTP/ IAS/IPS, we cannot see this setting. Therefore, we cannot schedule a provisioning job from IAS to CBC.

According to SAP documentation, IPS tenants created before 2022 are configured with SAP BTP Neo. Is that true?

What should we do in this case?
Is there any way for us to access our CBC demo system?
Or does SAP need to provide us with a new tenant?

 

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: AI-Assisted SAP Datasphere Modeling
entry.content:
Absolutely agree with @albertosimeoni .

The CSN file format is an absolute mess when you get to anything more advanced than simple local tables, and "manually adjusting" them is like running naked through a mine field: You're gonna get hurt, and you're gonna look pretty silly in the process. 

The CSN file for one of my simpler Analytical Models is 14000 lines long, and contains several spaces, views, tables and calculations. 

Why don't we try and focus our effort on the more complex objects? 
Creating local tables is not the bottleneck, I assure you.

entry.title: Display CIM Approval Comment in S/4HANA Public Cloud Approval Workflow
entry.content:
Dear Partner Community,

we are currently working with Central Invoice Management (CIM) together with S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Our customer currently has two approval workflows in place:

  • the standard approval workflow in Central Invoice Management, and

  • the standard approval workflow in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Our requirement is the following:
During the approval process in CIM, the approver should be able to enter a text/comment, and this text should then be visible in the approval workflow in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

At the moment, we are unsure about the best way to transfer or expose this text so that it is displayed during the approval step in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Has anyone implemented a similar scenario or has ideas on how this could be handled (e.g., via extensibility, workflow context, or integration options)?

Any insights or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Kind regards,
Philipp

entry.title: Re: Production Integration with Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @Matthias_G_ , sorry for the late reply.

This is controlled by the warehouse order creation rules - see here for documentation.

You could for example try to define a filter, leveraging the configuration option "Field to Limit Warehouse Order Size" to limit by the field "RDOCID Document ID". You then need to make sure you also sort the warehouse tasks accordingly. Please see the documentation of the configuration field itself for further information.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Streamlining Application Transport with SAP Cloud Transport Management on BTP
entry.content:
In today's multi-environment SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) landscape, managing application artifacts and content across Development, Test, and Production subaccounts can be challenging. SAP Cloud Transport Management (SAP CTM) provides a centralized, auditable solution for transporting deliverables across Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Neo environments.

This article explores the capabilities of SAP CTM and provides a comprehensive, step­ by-step guide for implementing an end-to-end transport setup for SAP Build projects

What is SAP Cloud Transport Management?

SAP Cloud Transport Management is a centralized service within SAP BTP that enables organizations to:

  • Manage software deliverables across multiple BTP environments
  • Transport content between different subaccounts (Development, Test, Production)
  • Maintain comprehensive audit trails of all changes
  • Enable separation of concerns between development and operations teams

This separation allows developers to trigger transports from their development environment, while a central operations team manages imports into test and production environments, ensuring proper governance and control.

Supported Content Types

SAP CTM supports a wide range of content types across various BTP services and solutions. Understanding content types is crucial: a content type is an entity you select when creating a new node in SAP CTM. The following table highlights some of the most used services and their transportable content:

SAP BTP Service/ SAP Cloud Solution

Content

Content Type

Environment

Content Agent Service

Joule Studio in SAP Build

Joule Studio projects

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes (for import)

SAP Analytics Cloud

Content network packages

Application Content

Cloud Foundry / Neo

No

SAP API Management

API artifacts

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes (for content selection and deployment)

SAP Asset Performance Management

Communication system configurations

Coding masks

Risk and criticality assessment templates

Strategy assessment
templates

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Batch Release Hub for Life Sciences

Configuration settings

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP BTP ABAP environment

References to SAP BTP ABAP environment content

BTP ABAP

SAP BTP ABAP environment

n/a

SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

HTML5 apps/ Java apps/ Node Js apps

MTA

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP BTP, Neo application runtime

Destinations/ HTML 5 roles/ HTML5 apps/ Java apps/ SAP Fiori apps/ SAP Fiori Portal/ SAP Fiori roles/ Authorization groups

MTA

Neo

n/a

SAP Build Apps (part of SAP Build)

Authorization groups

MTA

Neo

n/a

SAP Build Apps (part of SAP Build)

Projects

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes (for export and import)

SAP Build Process Automation

Projects

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes (for export and import)

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Content

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Content

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Datasphere

Content packages

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Destination Service

Destinations

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes

SAP Digital Manufacturing

Production process designs

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Entitlement Management

Configuration content

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Excise Tax Management

Warehouses

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Group Reporting Data Collection

Forms and folders

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP HANA Deployment Infrastructure (HDI)

HDI content

MTA

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP HANA XS classic model

Delivery units (DU)

XSC DU

Neo

n/a

SAP Integration Suite

Content

MTA

Neo

n/a

SAP Integration Suite

Integration packages (integration, flows, value mappings, APIs)

MTA

Cloud Foundry

Yes (for content selection and deployment)

SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management

Configuration settings

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Mobile Services

Application configurations

MTA

 

Yes

SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model

Environments

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

In-house business content (controls, automated procedures, manual procedures and regulations)

Application Content

Cloud Foundry

No

Note: Multitarget Application Archives (MTAs) are packages containing cloud applications or content created within cloud applications. For example, SAP Cloud Integration content is packaged and transported as an MTA.

Transport Architecture: Three-Subaccount Model

The recommended architecture for SAP CTM implementation involves four distinct subaccounts [Dev, Test, Prod and Central], each serving a specific purpose:

Source Subaccount (Development)

  • Primary development environment where SAP Build Apps projects are created and edited
  • Developers have full editing capabilities

Target Subaccount (Test/Production)

  • Destination environment for transported content
  • SAP Build Apps projects are view-only in these tenants, ensuring controlled deployment

Central Subaccount (Transport Management)

  • Administrative hub for managing content transport between Source and Target tenants
    Fig: Content Flow Architecture

Step-by-Step Implementation Guide

This section provides a detailed walkthrough for setting up SAP Cloud Transport Management to transport SAP Build projects from Development to Test environments.

Prerequisites

Source Subaccount (Development):

  • Enable Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Subscribe to SAP Build Apps
  • Subscribe to Content Agent Service
  • Create an instance of Content Agent Service
  • Generate a service key for the Content Agent instance
    Fig: Check Cloud Foundry Subscription

Fig: Check Content Agent Service and SAP Build Process Automation Subscription

Fig: Check Service Key in Development System

Target Subaccount (Test):

  • Enable Cloud Foundry environment
  • Subscribe to SAP Build Apps
  • Subscribe to Content Agent Service
  • Create an instance of Content Agent Service
  • Generate a service key for the Content Agent instance

Central Subaccount:

  • Subscribe to Cloud Transport Management Service (Documentation),
  • Create an instance of Cloud Transport Management (Documentation),
  • Generate or obtain a service key from an administrator (Documentation)

Fig: Subscribe to Cloud Transport Management and Create Service Key

Configuring BTP Destinations

Step 1: Configure TransportManagementService Destination (Source Subaccount)

In your Development (Source) subaccount, create a destination with the following configuration (Documentation)

Property

Value

Name

TransportManagementService (case sensitive)

Type

HTTP

Description

mention relevant description

URL

uri from CTMS service key details

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

Client ID

uaa → clientid from CTMS service key details

Client Secret

uaa → clientsecret from CTMS service key details

Token Service URL Type

Dedicated

Token Service URL

uaa → url from CTMS service key details + /oauth/token

Additional properties

sourceSystemId = DEV (this will be the name of Source node in CTMS)
sourceSystemId.sap.build = DEV

Fig: Mention the highlighted fields in the Service Key creation

Verification: Navigate to the Content Agent user interface in your Source tenant. A green "Connected" status in the Cloud Transport Management tile confirms successful configuration.

Fig: Check Connection Status

Step 2: Configure Content Agent Destinations (Central Subaccount)

In your Central subaccount, create destinations pointing to each Target tenant (Test, Production) [Documentation]:

Property

Value

Name

Pick names that have the tenant role in them, for example BUILD_TEST and BUILD_PROD

Type

HTTP

Description

 

URL

url from CAS service key details

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

Client ID

uaa → clientid from CAS service key details

Client Secret

uaa → clientsecret from CAS service key details

Token Service URL Type

Dedicated

Token Service URL

uaa → url from CAS service key details + /oauth/token

 

Setting Up the Transport Landscape

Access the Cloud Transport Management application in your Central subaccount to configure the transport landscape.

Step 1: Create Source Node (DEV)

  • Open Cloud Transport Management application
  • Navigate to Transport Nodes
  • Create a new node with name "DEV"
  • Select appropriate content type (e.g., MTA for SAP Build Apps)

Fig: Transport node in development subaccount

Step 2: Create Target Node (TEST)

  • Create another node with name "TEST"
  • Configure with matching content type

Fig: Transport Node in test Subaccount

Step 3: Create Transport Route

  • Navigate to Transport Routes
  • Create a new route from "DEV" to "TEST"
  • Activate the route

Fig: Transport Route from Development to Test

Executing the Transport

Step 1: Initiate Transport from Development

  • Open SAP Build Process Automation application in your development tenant
  • Select the project version you want to transport
  • Click the Transport button to create a transport request

Fig: Transport the project from Development to Test i n SAP Build

Step 2: Review of Cloud Transport Management

  • Open Cloud Transport Management application in Central tenant
  • Verify the transport request appears in the queue

Important Note: If the "Transport Operator" role is not assigned to your user in the Central tenant, the Import option will be disabled. Ensure the "Import Operator" role is properly assigned before proceeding.

Fig: Check if the Import option is reflecting in CTMS

Step 3: Import Transport Request

  • Select the transport request
  • Click Import to deploy to the TEST node
  • Monitor the import progress

Fig: Check Status of import

 

Step 4: Verify in Target Environment

  • Open SAP Build Apps in your Test tenant
  • Check the import queue to confirm the transported content
    Fig: Check import queue in SAP Build

Step 5: Verify Import Completion

  •  After successful import, open SAP Build Apps in the Test tenant
  •  Verify that the project is now available and visible
  •  Confirm the project is in view-only mode (as expected for target environments

Fig: Import Project in SAP Build

Tip: You can configure additional transport routes following the same pattern to move content from Test to Production environments

Troubleshooting Common Issues

Issue 1: Connection Not Showing as "Connected" in Content Agent

Solution: Verify that all credentials in the TransportManagementService destination are correctly copied from the CTMS service key. Pay special attention to the Token Service URL format.

Issue 2: Import Option Disabled in CTMS

Solution: Ensure the "Import Operator" role is assigned to your user in the Central subaccount. Without this role, you'll only be able to view transport requests but not import them.

 

Issue 3: Transport Request Not Appearing in CTMS

Solution: Check that the sourcesystemld in the TransportManagementService destination matches exactly with the node name created in CTMS. This is case­ sensitive.


Conclusion

SAP Cloud Transport Management provides a robust, centralized solution for managing application lifecycle across SAP BTP environments. By implementing the three­ subaccount architecture and following the configuration steps outlined in this guide, organizations can establish a controlled, auditable transport process that ensures quality and governance throughout the development lifecycle.

The separation of concerns between development and operations teams, combined with comprehensive audit trails, makes SAP CTM an essential tool for enterprises managing complex SAP BTP landscapes. Whether you're working with SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, Integration Suite, or any of the supported content types, SAP CTM streamlines your deployment process while maintaining the control and visibility needed for production environments.

By following this guide, you now have a complete understanding of how to set up and execute transports between Development, Test, and Production environments, enabling efficient and governed application delivery across your SAP BTP landscape.

entry.title: Re: Missing Depreciation Area in Asset Master despite Com...
entry.content:
Good day @Wayne10 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is a Public Cloud restriction. A depreciation area can be configured at company code level, but it will not appear in the asset master if you do not assign the related valuation view or it is inactive for the asset class. See KBA 3696205 for more details. In other words:

  • Company code configuration defines what is technically available.
  • Asset master display depends on which valuation views that are active for the asset class, so inactive valuation views hide their depreciation areas.

Here is what I suggest:

  • In the Accounting Principle Assignment to Ledger and Company Code activity, verify that the ledger group 2L is actually assigned to US company code. See KBA 3696205 for more details.

If 2L is not assigned, then valuation views for 2L will not be available for activation in the asset class, and depreciation areas that are tied to that valuation view will not be visible.

  • In SSCUI 102609, open asset class 1100 for US compan code, check if there is a valuation view that corresponds to the ledger group behind Area 16. If it is missing or inactive, activate or add it according to what the activity allows in your tenant.
  • After activating the valuation view for the asset class, create a new test asset and check Valuation again. The reason is, if the area was inactive at the time, you create it, older assets may not show it the same way, depending on the scenario and the specific valuation view setup.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: WSSE error "The security token could not be authenticated or authorized" in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello All,
I am currently working on an integration flow where SOAP 1.x adapter with WS-Security configuration is used in combination with a request-reply step. The request execution completes successfully but after the response generation phase the request-reply step fails with the following error - "org.apache.cxf.binding.soap.SoapFault: The security token could not be authenticated or authorized, cause: org.apache.wss4j.common.ext.WSSecurityException: The security token could not be authenticated or authorized"

I would like to understand if some kind of validation is occurring for the response payload at the SAP CPI side and is that why it is failing and that is there any mechanism to bypass this validation? Also would like to get clarity on another thing - in the response payload in the binary security token, valuetype attribute is PKIPathv1, which means the complete certificate chain is bundled together for signing purpose. But in SAP cpi we have uploaded the certificates separately as leaf, intermediate and root. Can this be a cause? 


When trace is enabled, I am able to see the SOAP response payload in the message logs. However, I would like to understand how the response payload can be captured within the exception subprocess when this failure occurs. I tried using ${in.body}, but it provided a blank body.
Has anyone encountered a similar scenario or have suggestions on how to capture the response payload in the exception subprocess?
Kindly see the below attachments for reference.
